intro: FARMHAND: Oh. A kid. We get about one a year. Welcome to the farm, I guess. The mons live here. They come and go as they please. Mostly they please to stay.
FARMHAND: Some company keeps papers on every lot in town, I hear. Not this one. Nobody ever filed us. I couldn't tell you why. The mons seem to like it that way.
FARMHAND: You want one? Wild ones are up the road. Rough one up till it's winded. Not mean, just tired. Then TALK to it.
FARMHAND: And when it asks you things, actually listen. Answer like yourself. They can tell. I don't know how. They just can.
FARMHAND: If it likes what it hears, it comes back here and waits for you. That's the whole arrangement. No signing. Go on, the grass is that way.
tutorialCatch: FARMHAND: There's a little one hanging around the fence post just up the road. Cheerful sort. Asks easy questions. Good one to start on.
FARMHAND: Wear it down first or it won't sit still long enough to chat. And if it runs off, don't sulk. That one always wanders back.
FARMHAND: Go say hi. Or fight, then hi. That's the order, for whatever reason.
firstCompanion: FARMHAND: Taking this one along, then. ...Good pick. It picked you too, for the record. Nobody keeps the record. But it did.
FARMHAND: Feed it whatever you eat. Let it sleep near you. Bring it back if it gets banged up. That's all the instructions there are.
FARMHAND: The others will watch the road for you two. They do that. Nobody asked them to.
fusionUnlock: FARMHAND: Two of them now, huh. Okay. Come around back of the barn. There's a thing you should see before you ever need it.
FARMHAND: This is the altar. It was here before the barn was. Put two mons on it and you get one mon. A new one. Stronger, usually.
FARMHAND: The two you put in don't come back. The new one remembers things it shouldn't. A trick one of them knew. A way of standing. So it's not nothing. But it's not them.
FARMHAND: I don't tell folks to use it or not to. I just tell them how it works. Now you know. Lunch is at noon.
idleTips: FARMHAND: That Munch fella's SPY trick works on wild ones, you know. Tells you what sort of heart you're arguing with. Handy.
FARMHAND: Gave a wrong answer out there, did you. Good. Watch how the mon takes it. That's it telling you who it is. Cheaper than asking.
FARMHAND: Pet them when you visit. They keep count. Not in a ledger way. In a dog way.
FARMHAND: The ones that stay here toughen up slow. Watching the road, eating regular. It adds up. Slower than fighting, but nobody gets hit.
FARMHAND: Letting one go is forever, mind. They don't circle back. They're not lost. They're just elsewhere. Big difference. Took me years.
FARMHAND: The strong ones ask harder questions. Stands to reason. More to lose by picking the wrong company.
release: FARMHAND: Letting this one go? ...Alright. Stand still a second and let it look at you. They like to be sure it's on purpose.
The mon looks at Bosch for a long moment. Then it walks up the road without hurrying. It does not look back. That's how you know it's fine.

handoverScene: The mon steps off the farm grass and stands next to Bosch. Like it has done it a hundred times.
It settles in on Bosch's left. Nobody told it to walk there. It just seemed to know the spot was open.
CLOAK: ...Hey. You. Stick close to Bosch, okay? Between us and whatever comes. That's the good spot. I'll take the outside.
MUNCH: Three people split a check weird. Four splits even. I'm just saying the math has been off for a while and now it isn't.
Nobody says anything for a second. The mon fills the quiet like it was shaped for it.
FARMHAND: Well. That's a party. Go on before I get sentimental. I don't have the face for it.
townLines: That one's not a milady. It blinked at me. On purpose.
It looked in our window and my husband waved at it. He hasn't waved at anything in a year.
I asked it who it worked for. It sniffed my shoe and moved on. Refreshing, honestly.
Most faces around here came from the same place. Yours brought its own. Keep it close.
My daughter petted it and it let her. Then nobody tried to sell her anything. I keep waiting for the catch and it just doesn't come.

journal.meet-farmhand: Find the lot with no paperwork. Talk to the FARMHAND.
journal.first-catch: Wear a wild mon down, then CONVINCE it. Listen for real.
journal.first-companion: Pick a mon at the farm to walk with you.
journal.first-fusion: Bring two mons to the altar behind the barn.

monEncounterHint: The wild mon is winded. It's looking at Bosch like it has questions. Bosch could try to CONVINCE it.
momPhone: MOM: A mon? Well. Does it eat people food? Set out a bowl anyway. Everybody likes a bowl with their name on it.
MOM: Tell it hello from me. And honey, wash your hands after the petting. Not before. After. Love you. Don't run out of clean socks.
